assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies the clusters as '2 prod clusters | Autopilot | Stable channel' and mentions using Rapid channel dev clusters for testing first

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions no SSH access, mandatory resource requests, automatic node upgrades, and limited troubleshooting options in Autopilot-specific notes section

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: While mentions checking GKE deprecation insights dashboard, it doesn't provide specific kubectl commands for checking deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'ALL containers have resource requests (CPU/memory) - mandatory in Autopilot' and includes validation commands for resource request violations

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Pre-upgrade checklist includes PDBs configuration, no bare pods requirement, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds limits

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands to verify control plane version for both clusters

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Includes comprehensive workload health checks like deployment replica counts, pod status, StatefulSets, and application health checks

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Rapid channel dev clusters already upgraded to 1.30 and validated' and mentions using dev clusters as testing ground in the final note
Post-upgrade checklist includes metrics pipeline, Cloud Logging, error rates, latency baselines, and resource throttling events
